what_it_does: Waits for a local OpenAI-compatible server on :8000, scans its serve log for CUDA OOM or Traceback errors, then counts distinct vi_sync_* identifiers in a requirements markdown file and calls the local /v1/chat/completions endpoint with a JSON-schema response format to extract structured data, finally comparing the vi_sync_* identifiers present in the LLM output against those in the source file.
observations: Polls localhost:8000/v1/models up to 40 times with 8-second sleeps
Checks ~/vllm-tests/serve_scan_restart.log for 'CUDA out of memory' or 'Traceback'
Uses a hardcoded Windows/WSL absolute ROOT path under /mnt/c/Users/Workstation001
Imports an external Python module 'capture2' for SYS and SCHEMA constants
Sends the entire requirements file body as the user message
Counts and prints vi_sync_* token sets from both input and output
standouts: It is a local-LLM extraction fidelity checker that compares identifier preservation before and after JSON-schema generation
Combines bash server health polling with an inline Python OpenAI-style API call
Depends on an undocumented capture2 module and a running local vLLM/OpenAI-compatible server
concerns: Hardcoded personal filesystem path and model name
No error handling around the Python request, JSON parse, or capture2 import
Log scan only looks for two specific error strings and ignores other failure modes
No explicit pass/fail assertion; only prints counts and sections
Special characters in the requirements body are inserted directly into JSON without escaping
notes: A quick one-off validation harness that health-checks a local inference server and measures whether a quantized model preserves vi_sync_* identifiers when extracting structured data from a requirements file.
questions: What do the vi_sync_* identifiers represent and why must they be preserved?
What are the contents of capture2.SYS and capture2.SCHEMA?
Why was 'cyankiwi/Qwen3.5-4B-AWQ-4bit' chosen for this comparison?
What threshold determines success or failure for identifier preservation?
Is this script intended for one-time debugging or repeated regression testing?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose appears to be validating that an LLM extraction preserves vi_sync_* identifiers from a requirements file; the script actually performs a health check, counts tokens, calls the model, and prints a comparison without an explicit pass/fail verdict.
intention_signals: The script seems to be trying to verify that a locally served quantized model can faithfully extract structured requirements into a JSON schema while retaining every vi_sync_* token from the source document.
novelty: It uses a simple grep-based token count as a lightweight fidelity metric before and after JSON-schema-constrained LLM extraction.
contribution: A reusable pattern for polling a local inference server, scanning its log for OOM errors, and comparing token-level preservation between a source document and a JSON-schema-constrained LLM output.
summary_for_embedding: A bash-and-inline-Python script that polls a local OpenAI-compatible server, checks its log for CUDA errors, and compares vi_sync_* identifier preservation between a requirements markdown file and a JSON-schema LLM extraction.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to validate that a local quantized model preserves all vi_sync_* identifiers when extracting structured data from a requirements document.
